question: About the job Snowflake is about empowering enterprises to achieve their full potential — and people too. With a culture that’s all in on impact, innovation, and collaboration, Snowflake is the sweet spot for building big, moving fast, and taking technology — and careers — to the next level.  Snowflake is a global platform where thousands of organizations store and process their data. Our Data Marketplace allows our customers to exchange data across geographies, organizationions and cloud providers. We are now building an App Marketplace on top of the Data Marketplace. Think of it as an App Store or Play Store but with the apps running on the Snowflake Cloud, not phones. Snowflake is becoming an application platform. It’s a new, big, and differentiating feature for Snowflake and our customers.  The Native Apps team in Toronto is in the center of this innovation. We’re working on the core building blocks for the apps to support the app lifecycle - creation, versioning, global rollouts, installation and execution in a secure sandbox. We enable app runtimes for multiple languages and execution models. We deal with many aspects of the app framework from access control and permissions, through IP protection to UI frameworks. We’re still early in our journey and we are looking for talented and passionate engineers to innovate with us. Our big, multi-year goal is to launch a hugely successful app ecosystem. We count not only on your deep tech expertise and problem solving skills but also on eagerness to evangelize the new platform among developers.  AS A SOFTWARE ENGINEER FOR NATIVE APPS, YOU WILL:  Drive high impact initiatives that span our product areas and tech stack including Java , python and golang Promote strong collaboration within a cross functional team, including design, product management. Deliver features requiring close coordination with cross-functional teams Be a strong contributor to the product vision and drive team planning. Build for scale and high performance.   OUR IDEAL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE:  2+ years hands-on software development experience, including significant experience in backend software development  Deep understanding of distributed design architecture, good API design patterns, security, performance and scale. Knowledge of data development tools, data infrastructure, or ML is a plus. You go to work everyday striving to be the very best you can be. You're undaunted by ambiguity and are motivated to drive impact.   ABOUT APPS AND SNOWFLAKE:  Snowflake Native Apps Snowflake for Applications and Collaboration Snowflake Marketplace Life at Snowflake Canada   Our Human Resources Department may use artificial intelligence to support its administrative processes.  This role is available and may represent a new position.  This role is exempt from Ontario’s job posting requirements.  Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.  How do you want to make your impact?  For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com  This role is exempt from Ontario’s job posting requirements. Compensation information is provided voluntarily to promote transparency and consistency across our job postings.
reply:

question: Job Description  What is the opportunity?  Are you a talented, creative, and results-driven professional who thrives on delivering high-performing applications. Come join us!  As part of the CDO Shared Data Management Office (SDMO) team, the Software Developer will design, develop, test, and maintain high-performance software applications and data infrastructure that support enterprise-wide data management and AI initiatives. You will work in a cross-functional agile environment, collaborating with data architects, analysts, and data scientists to deliver solutions that enable intelligent data governance, seamless data integration, and ML/AI enablement across the organization.  What will you do?  Design and develop enterprise-grade data pipelines, ETL/ELT processes, and APIs  Build and optimize data infrastructure components that support real-time analytics, reporting, and AI/ML implementation into pipelines Implement data quality monitoring, validation, and lineage tracking systems to ensure data integrity across the organization Develop microservices and containerized applications (Docker, Kubernetes) for scalable data processing and integration Write clean, well-documented, and testable code following enterprise standards and best practices Participate in code reviews, performance optimization, and technical design discussions Support CI/CD pipeline development and automation for data platform deployments Troubleshoot production issues and implement solutions with minimal downtime  What do you need to succeed?  Must-have  3+ years of experience and strong proficiency in one or more programming languages (Python, Spring, Java, Scala, Go) Advanced SQL and experience with relational and NoSQL databases Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms (AWS, Azure) or on-premises data warehouse solutions Familiarity with analytical and cloud data lakehouse platforms (Databricks, Snowflake) Understanding of data modeling, ETL patterns, and data governance principles Experience with containerization, orchestration, and deployment technologies (Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform) Knowledge of ML/AI workflows, model deployment, and inference pipelines Proficiency with version control systems (Git) and CI/CD tools Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently with minimum supervision Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate across technical and business teams  Nice to have:  Experience with data mesh or decentralized data architecture patterns Familiarity with streaming technologies (Kafka, Apache Flink) Knowledge of data security, encryption, and compliance frameworks (GDPR, HIPAA) Exposure to observability and monitoring tools (Grafana, Datadog, Prometheus, ELK stack) Agile/Scrum development experience Background in machine learning operations (MLOps) or data engineering best practices

question: can you tell me about aman experience in raintree systems
reply:
Aman Kumar worked at Raintree Systems Inc as a Backend Software Engineer from May 2021 to September 2022. During his tenure, he:

* Built and maintained distributed Spring Boot microservices with Hibernate/JPA for ORM, designing 15+ RESTful APIs and GraphQL endpoints, achieving a 65% reduction in query execution time.
* Designed 30+ AWS Lambda functions with shared Lambda layers for cross-service reuse, increasing processing speed by 35% and reusability by 52%, resulting in $1,000 cost savings per large-scale deployment.
* Containerized Spring Boot applications using Docker and orchestrated deployments on AWS EC2 via Node.js CI/CD scripts, reducing deployment time by 40% and achieving 99.9% uptime across production services.
* Implemented 15+ serverless workflows using AWS Step Functions with REST API integrations, enabling reliable orchestration across 5+ backend microservices and eliminating manual handoff errors.
* Delivered end-to-end backend development of Spring Boot microservices and REST APIs integrated with PostgreSQL, reducing project delivery time by 15%.
* Achieved a 98% unit test pass rate using JUnit and Mockito, improving reliability of large-scale codebases.
* Diagnosed and resolved production defects across distributed systems, reducing downtime by 20%.
* Collaborated with stakeholders and engineers in Agile ceremonies to deliver scalable enterprise solutions.
* Spearheaded backend development for Epayrent (150K+ Play Store downloads), building secure Spring Boot microservices and REST APIs that improved system response time by 20% and increased user engagement by 30%.
* Implemented JWT-based authentication with Spring Security and designed a centralized logging framework, reducing debugging time by 25% while improving API monitoring and reliability.
* Mentored junior developers in Spring Boot and microservices architecture and collaborated in Agile sprints to deliver production-ready features.

Aman also had a leadership experience as a Technical Mentor & Initiative Lead at Raintree Systems Inc, where he:

* Accelerated team delivery capacity by mentoring 2 software engineering interns over 4 months, cutting onboarding time from 8 weeks to ~5 weeks (35% faster) through structured backend training in Spring Boot, microservices, and distributed systems design.
* Designed weekly learning modules covering Spring Boot, microservices architecture, and REST API design, enabling interns to independently deliver 6+ production-ready features (including a reporting API and async notification service) within their first 60 days, contributing directly to a Q3 product release milestone.
